chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.D-4218 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Fresh Case 1. For orders on Misc. No.17404 of 2025 (Urgent) 2. For orders on office objection No.1 to 6. 3. For orders on Misc. No.17405 of 2025 (exemption) 4. For orders on Misc. No.17406/2025 (Stay). 5. For hearing of main case. 27.08.2025 Mr. Farhan Khaliq Anwer, Advocates for the petitioner. *************** 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption is allowed subject to all just exceptions. 4-5. It is stated by the learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner is presently in Dubai, UAE, and has filed the instant petition through his advocate, seeking protective/transitory bail in FIR No. 879 of 2020, registered under Sections 489-F and 420 PPC at Police Station Gulistan-e-Jauhar, Karachi. The petitioner contends that his name has been mentioned in the FIR; therefore, he seeks protection from arrest upon his return to Pakistan at Jinnah International Airport, Karachi, in order to surrender before the learned trial Court. It is further stated that the learned trial Court, vide order dated 26.08.2022, directed NADRA and the concerned authorities to block the CNIC and passport of the petitioner; hence, he is unable to travel and to appear before the learned trial Court. Without touching the merits of the case, the petitioner, Shahid Masood Abbasi son of Masood Ahmed Abbasi is admitted to protective/transitory bail for a period of 15 [Fifteen] days from today subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.500,000/- (Rupees 2 Five Hundred Thousand Only) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court.

It is further stated that the learned trial Court, vide order dated 26.08.2022, directed NADRA and the concerned authorities to block the CNIC and passport of the petitioner; hence, he is unable to travel and to appear before the learned trial Court. Without touching the merits of the case, the petitioner, Shahid Masood Abbasi son of Masood Ahmed Abbasi is admitted to protective/transitory bail for a period of 15 [Fifteen] days from today subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.500,000/- (Rupees 2 Five Hundred Thousand Only) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. This concession of bail shall remain operative only for fifteen days from today viz. 27.08.2025 and shall cease to exist on 10.09.2025 or before it when the petitioner surrenders before the trial Court, whichever is earlier. NADRA as well as Passport & Immigration Department, Government of Pakistan are directed to unblock the CNIC and Passport of the petitioner for the above said period. In case the petitioner shall not approach the learned trial court within the prescribed period, his surety shall be forfeited and CNIC and Passport will be blocked automatically. Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Hyder/PS
